name: eOther.01
label: Review Requested
definition: Indication of whether the PCR needs review by anyone.

name: eOther.02
label: Potential System of Care/Specialty/Registry Patient
definition: An indication if the patient may meet the entry criteria for an injury or illness specific registry.

name: eOther.03
label: Personal Protective Equipment Used
definition: The personal protective equipment which was used by EMS personnel during this EMS patient contact.

name: eOther.04
label: EMS Professional (Crew Member) ID
definition: The ID number of the EMS Crew Member associated with eOther.03, eOther.05, eOther.06.
element_comments: Added to allow documentation of PPE and work related health and safety down to the individual level within each EMS crew.
version3_changes: Added to better document EMS workplace health and safety issues.

name: eOther.05
label: Suspected EMS Work Related Exposure, Injury, or Death
official_name: Suspected EMS Work Related Exposure, Injury, or Death
definition: Indication of an EMS work related exposure, injury, or death associated with this EMS event.
element_comments: Associated with eOther.04 (EMS Professional (Crew Member) ID)

validation_rules.message: When Suspected EMS Work Related Exposure, Injury, or Death is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Suspected EMS Work Related Exposure, Injury, or Death has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eOther.06
label: The Type of Work-Related Injury, Death or Suspected Exposure
official_name: The Type of Work-Related Injury, Death or Suspected Exposure
definition: The type of EMS crew member work-related injury, death, or suspected exposure related to the EMS response.

allowed_values.display: Exposure-Body Fluid Contact to Broken Skin
Exposure-Body Fluid Contact with Intact Skin
Exposure-Body Fluid Contact with Mucosal Surface
Exposure-Needle Stick with Body Fluid Injection
Exposure-Needle Stick without Body Fluid Injection
Violence-Physical Attack or Attempt to Attack
Violence-Unwelcome Sexual Attention, Remarks, Jokes or Gestures
Violence-Unwanted Touching of a Sexual Nature

element_comments: Associated witheOther.04 (EMS Professional (Crew Member) ID).

validation_rules.message: When The Type of Work-Related Injury, Death or Suspected Exposure is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When The Type of Work-Related Injury, Death or Suspected Exposure has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When The Type of Work-Related Injury, Death or Suspected Exposure has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.

name: eOther.07
label: Natural, Suspected, Intentional, or Unintentional Disaster
official_name: Natural, Suspected, Intentional, or Unintentional Disaster
definition: Event caused by natural forces or Suspected and Intentional/Unintentional Disasters (terrorism).
element_comments: Based on FEMA website

name: eOther.08
label: Crew Member Completing this Report
definition: The statewide assigned ID number of the EMS crew member which completed this patient care report.
element_comments: If the EMS Professional has a specific ID associated with two states, the ID used would typically be based on the EMS Agencies state license.

validation_rules.message: When Crew Member Completing this Report is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Crew Member Completing this Report has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eOther.09
label: External Electronic Document Type
definition: Document type which has been electronically stored with PCR.
element_comments: Added to allow an improved implementation of electronic healthcare records.
version3_changes: Added to allow an improved implementation of electronic healthcare records.

name: eOther.10
label: File Attachment Type
definition: The description of the file attachment stored in File Attachment Image (eOther.11).
element_comments: The description is defined as the extension of the file type. Examples of file name extensions include "doc", "jpeg", "tiff", etc. For a list of additional extensions reference the following website(s): 3D Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/3d_image Raster Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/raster_image Vector Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/vector_image Video formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/video Audio formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/audio Comparison of formats: Image: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_image_file_formats Audio: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Audio_file_format Container: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_container_formats A general list of image formats: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image_file_formats Mime Types: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_media_type
version3_changes: Added to allow an improved implementation of electronic healthcare records.

name: eOther.11
label: File Attachment Image
definition: The file that is attached electronically to the patient care report.
element_comments: Associated with eOther.09 (External Electronic Documents) and eOther.10 (File Attachment Type).
version3_changes: Added to allow an improved implementation of electronic healthcare records.

name: eOther.12
label: Type of Person Signing
definition: The individual's signature associated with eOther.15 (Signature Status).

allowed_values.display: EMS Primary Care Provider (for this event)

version3_changes: Added to better document disposition and billing information.

name: eOther.13
label: Signature Reason
definition: The reason for the individuals signature.

name: eOther.14
label: Type Of Patient Representative
definition: If Patient Representative is chosen as the owner of the signature, this documents the relationship of the individual signing to the patient.

allowed_values.display: Other Care Provider (Home health, hospice, etc.)

element_comments: Required for Billing.
version3_changes: Added to improve documentation on disposition and billing.

name: eOther.15
label: Signature Status
definition: Indication that the signature for the Type of Person Signing and Signature Reason has been collected or attempted to be collected.

allowed_values.display: Not Signed - Crew Called out to another call
Not Signed - Due to Distress Level
Not Signed - In Law Enforcement Custody
Not Signed - Physical Impairment of Extremities
Not Signed - Transferred Care/No Access to Obtain Signature

version3_changes: Added to better document disposition and billing.

name: eOther.16
label: Signature File Name
definition: The name of the graphic file for the signature.

name: eOther.17
label: Signature File Type
definition: The description of the file attachment stored in Signature Graphic (eOther.18).
element_comments: The description is defined as the extension of the file type. Examples of file name extensions include "doc", "jpeg", "tiff", etc. For a list of additional extensions reference the following website(s): 3D Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/3d_image Raster Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/raster_image Vector Image formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/vector_image Video formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/video Audio formats: http://www.fileinfo.com/filetypes/audio Comparison of formats: Image: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_image_file_formats Audio: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Audio_file_format Container: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_container_formats A general list of image formats: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Image_file_formats Mime Types: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_media_type

name: eOther.18
label: Signature Graphic
definition: The graphic file for the signature.
version3_changes: Added to better document disposition and billing.

name: eOther.19
label: Date/Time of Signature
definition: The date and time the crew member, patient, patient representative, or facility representative signature was captured.
element_comments: DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration
version3_changes: Added to better document disposition and billing.

name: eOther.20
label: Signature Last Name
definition: The last name of the crew member, patient, patient representative, or facility representative associated with the signature group.
version3_changes: Added to better document disposition and billing.

name: eOther.21
label: Signature First Name
definition: The first name of the crew member, patient, patient representative, or facility representative associated with the signature group.

Discharged/transferred to another short term general hospital for inpatient care
Discharged/transferred to a skilled nursing facility (SNF)

name: eOutcome.03
label: External Report ID/Number Type
definition: The Type of External Report or Record associated with the Report/ID Number.
element_comments: This data element is a merge based on the following Version 2.2.1 elements: E22_03, E22_04, E22_05, and E22_06
version3_changes: Added to allow documentation of external record and identification numbers.

name: eOutcome.04
label: External Report ID/Number
definition: The ID or Number of the external report or record in eOutcome.03.
element_comments: This data element is a merge based on the following Version 2.2.1 elements: E22_03, E22_04, E22_05, and E22_06
version3_changes: Added to allow documentation of external record and identification numbers.

name: eOutcome.05
label: Other Report Registry Type
definition: The type of external report/registry that was documented as "other" in eOutcome.03
element_comments: This element should be used only when other-report or other-registry is used eOutcome.03.

name: eOutcome.09
label: Emergency Department Procedures
definition: The procedures performed on the patient during the emergency department visit.
element_comments: ICD-10-PCS has a seven character alphanumeric code structure. Each character contains up to 34 possible values. Each value represents a specific option for the general character definition (e.g., stomach is one of the values for the body part character). The ten digits 0-9 and the 24 letters A-H,J-N and P-Z may be used in each character. The letters O and I are not used in order to avoid confusion with the digits 0 and 1. Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Emergency Department Procedures is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Emergency Department Procedures has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

label: Emergency Department Diagnosis
definition: The practitioner's description of the condition or problem for which Emergency Department services were provided.
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM: Diagnosis Codes. Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Emergency Department Diagnosis is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).

name: eOutcome.11
label: Date/Time of Hospital Admission
definition: The date and time the patient was admitted to the hospital.
element_comments: DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

validation_rules.message: When Date/Time of Hospital Admission is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Date/Time of Hospital Admission has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

version3_changes: Added to better evaluate EMS care.

name: eOutcome.12
label: Hospital Procedures
definition: Hospital Procedures performed on the patient during the hospital admission.
element_comments: ICD-10-PCS has a seven character alphanumeric code structure. Each character contains up to 34 possible values. Each value represents a specific option for the general character definition (e.g., stomach is one of the values for the body part character). The ten digits 0-9 and the 24 letters A-H,J-N and P-Z may be used in each character. The letters O and I are not used in order to avoid confusion with the digits 0 and 1. Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Hospital Procedures is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Hospital Procedures has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

label: Injury Severity Score

element_comments: eCustomResults OeCustomResults.03 - CorrelationID of PatientCareReport Element or Group0 : 1 MeCustomResults.02 - Custom Element ID Referenced1 : 1 N, L, PMeCustomResults.01 - Custom Data Element Result1 : M CeCustomResults.ResultsGroup0 : M eCustomResults eCustomResults

name: ePatient.01
label: EMS Patient ID
definition: The unique ID for the patient within the Agency.
element_comments: This has been inserted to allow each patient to be tracked across multiple PCRs within an EMS Agency.

name: ePatient.02
label: Last Name
definition: The patient's last (family) name.
element_comments: Pertinent negatives accepted with option of "Unable to Complete" or "Refused".

validation_rules.message: When Last Name is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Last Name has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Last Name has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.03
label: First Name
definition: The patient's first (given) name.
element_comments: Pertinent negatives accepted with option of "Unable to Complete" or "Refused".

validation_rules.message: When First Name is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When First Name has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When First Name has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.04
label: Middle Initial/Name
definition: The patient's middle name, if any.

name: ePatient.05
label: Patient's Home Address
definition: Patient's address of residence.
element_comments: This is a CMS standard. According to the Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 15 - Ambulance, Ambulance suppliers bill using CMS-1500 form or CMS-1450 form for institution-based ambulance providers. This standard adheres to CMS-1500 and 1450. This element allows for a two line documentation of the address. For out of country addresses the second line should be used to document, city, country, postal code and any other pertinent information.

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Home Address is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Home Address has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.06
label: Patient's Home City
definition: The patient's primary city or township of residence.
element_comments: City codes are based on GNIS Feature Class. The primary Feature Class to use is "Civil" with "Populated Place" and "Military" code as additional options. Definitions for each GNIS City Feature Class can be found on the GNIS Codes website. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Home City is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Home City has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.07
label: Patient's Home County
definition: The patient's home county or parish of residence.
element_comments: Based on the ANSI Code Single Choice based on the County Name but stored as the ANSI code (combined 5 digit State and County codes) Should be required if there is a patient associated with the event. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Home County is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Home County has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Patient's Home County should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Patient's Home County should belong within the Patient's Home State.

name: ePatient.08
label: Patient's Home State
definition: The state, territory, or province where the patient resides.
element_comments: The ANSI Code Selection by text but stored as ANSI code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Home State is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Home State has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Patient's Home County should belong within the Patient's Home State.
Patient's Home State should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".

name: ePatient.09
label: Patient's Home ZIP Code
definition: The patient's ZIP code of residence.
element_comments: ZIP Codes Product Website:  https://www.zipcodedownload.com/Products/Product/Z5Commercial/Standard/Overview/ Product: USA - 5-digit ZIP Code Database, Commercial Edition

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Home ZIP Code is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Home ZIP Code has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Patient's Home ZIP Code should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".

name: ePatient.10
label: Patient's Country of Residence
definition: The country of residence of the patient.
element_comments: Based on the ISO Country Code. ANSI Country Codes (ISO 3166) Website: http://www.iso.org/iso/country_codes/iso_3166_code_lists.htm

name: ePatient.11
label: Patient Home Census Tract
definition: The census tract in which the patient lives.
element_comments: This data element was added to better document the service area of the EMS Agency. Each state listed is associated with the counties, census tracts, and ZIP codes within the EMS Agency Service Area for each state. The format of the census tract number must be an 11-digit number, based upon the 2010 census, using the pattern: 2-digit State Code 3-digit County Code 6-digit Census Tract Number (no decimal) Example: NEMSIS TAC office (UT, Salt Lake County, Census Tract - located at 295 Chipeta Way, Salt Lake City, UT) 49035101400 Census Tract Data Website (files and descriptions): https://www.census.gov/geographies/reference-files/2010/geo/relationship-files.html

name: ePatient.12
label: Social Security Number
definition: The patient's social security number.

validation_rules.message: When Social Security Number is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Social Security Number has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.13
label: Gender (DEPRECATED)
definition: The patient's gender. (DEPRECATED)

allowed_values.display: Other, neither exclusively male or female (DEPRECATED)

element_comments: Deprecated Comment The NEMSIS Program is funded by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration and this element was modified to comply with President Trump’s Executive Orders.

validation_rules.message: When Gender (DEPRECATED) is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Gender (DEPRECATED) has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: ePatient.14
label: Race
definition: The patient's race as defined by the OMB (US Office of Management and Budget).

allowed_values.display: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

element_comments: OMB requirements are provided at: . Using single multiple choice questionhttps://www.federalregister.gov/d/2024-06469 methodology to improve the completion of ethnicity information. Ethnicity (Version 2.2.1: E06_13) has been merged with this data element and retired.

validation_rules.message: When Race is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Race has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Race has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
When Race has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Race has a Pertinent Negative, no other value should be recorded.
Race should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".

name: ePatient.15
label: Age
definition: The patient's age (either calculated from date of birth or best approximation) at the time of the incident.

validation_rules.message: When Age is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Age has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Age should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Age Units should be recorded when Age is recorded.
When Age has a Pertinent Negative of "Approximate", it should have a value and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.16
label: Age Units
definition: The unit used to define the patient's age.

validation_rules.message: When Age Units is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Age Units has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Age Units should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Age Units should be recorded when Age is recorded.

name: ePatient.17
label: Date of Birth
definition: The patient's date of birth.

validation_rules.message: When Date of Birth is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Date of Birth has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Date of Birth has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePatient.18
label: Patient's Phone Number
definition: The patient's phone number.
element_comments: This element contains an attribute to define what type of phone number is being documented (e.g., Fax, Home, Mobile, Pager, and Work).

validation_rules.message: When Patient's Phone Number is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient's Phone Number has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
When Patient's Phone Number has a Pertinent Negative, no other value should be recorded.

name: ePatient.19
label: Patient's Email Address
definition: The email address of the patient.
element_comments: Added to improve follow-up and billing communication.

name: ePatient.20
label: State Issuing Driver's License
definition: The state that issued the drivers license.
element_comments: Stored as the ANSI State Code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

name: ePatient.21
label: Driver's License Number
definition: The patient's drivers license number.

name: ePatient.22
label: Alternate Home Residence
definition: Documentation of the type of patient without a home ZIP/Postal Code.

validation_rules.message: When Alternate Home Residence is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Alternate Home Residence has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: ePatient.23
label: Name Suffix
definition: The patient's name component following family name that may be used to describe a person's position in a family.

name: ePatient.24
label: Patient's Preferred Language(s)
definition: The patient's preferred language(s).

name: ePatient.25
label: Sex
definition: The patient's sex.

validation_rules.message: When Sex is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Sex has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Sex has a Pertinent Negative, it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: ePayment.01
label: Primary Method of Payment
definition: The primary method of payment or type of insurance associated with this EMS encounter.

validation_rules.message: When Primary Method of Payment is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Primary Method of Payment has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: ePayment.02
label: Physician Certification Statement
definition: Indication of whether a physician certification statement (PCS) is available documenting the medical necessity or the EMS encounter.
element_comments: Data Element Name changed from Certificate of Medical Necessity to Physician Certification Statement. The PCS statement and documentation of medical necessity is a requirement of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services. The circumstances are defined in 42 CFR 410.40 (d)(2) and (d)(3). http://www.cms.gov/ A PCS is required for the following non-emergency transport: 1) Non-Emergency Repetitive Scheduled 2) Non Emergency Non-Repetitive-Scheduled 3) Non Emergency Services - Non Scheduled - Under direct care of a physician

name: ePayment.03
label: Date Physician Certification Statement Signed
definition: The date the Physician Certification Statement was signed.
element_comments: Added to assist in billing documentation. Associated with ePayment.02: Physician Certification Statement. DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

name: ePayment.04
label: Reason for Physician Certification Statement
definition: The reason for EMS transport noted on the Physician Certification Statement.

allowed_values.display: Danger to self or others-seclusion (flight risk)
DVT requires elevation of lower extremity
Moderate to severe pain on movement
Morbid Obesity requires additional personnel/equipment to handle
Orthopedic device (backboard, halo, use of pins in traction, etc.) requiring special handling in transit
Restraints (Physical or Chemical) anticipated or used during transport
Risk of falling off wheelchair or stretcher while in motion (not related to obesity)
Severe Muscular weakness and de-conditioned state precludes any significant physical activity
Third Party assistance/attendant required to apply, administer, or regulate or adjust oxygen en route
Unable to maintain erect sitting position in a chair for time needed to transport, due to moderate muscular weakness and de-conditioning.
Unable to sit in chair or wheelchair due to Grade II or greater decubitus ulcers on buttocks.

element_comments: Added to assist in billing documentation.

name: ePayment.05
label: Healthcare Provider Type Signing Physician Certification Statement
official_name: Healthcare Provider Type Signing Physician Certification Statement
definition: The type of healthcare provider who signed the Physician Certification Statement.
element_comments: CMS defines the individual signing the PCS as a person who has personal knowledge of the beneficiary's condition at the time the ambulance transport is ordered or the service is furnished.

name: ePayment.06
label: Last Name of Individual Signing Physician Certification Statement
official_name: Last Name of Individual Signing Physician Certification Statement
definition: The last name of the healthcare provider who signed the Physician Certification Statement.
element_comments: Added to assist in billing documentation.

name: ePayment.07
label: First Name of Individual Signing Physician Certification Statement
official_name: First Name of Individual Signing Physician Certification Statement
definition: The first name of the healthcare provider who signed the Physician Certification Statement.
element_comments: Added to assist in billing documentation.

name: ePayment.08
label: Patient Resides in Service Area
definition: An indication of whether the patient's current residence is within the EMS agency service area.

allowed_values.display: Not a Resident Within EMS Service Area

element_comments: Added to assist in billing documentation.

name: ePayment.09
label: Insurance Company ID
definition: The ID Number of the patient's insurance company.
element_comments: This element should be used as the Health Plan ID (HPID) field in X12. It is currently under development for X12 and will be a future release. To align with USCDI, consider using the Issuer Identification Number (IIN) or Processor Control Number (PCN).

name: ePayment.10
label: Insurance Company Name
definition: The name of the patient's insurance company.

name: ePayment.11
label: Insurance Company Billing Priority
definition: The billing priority or order for the insurance company.

name: ePayment.12
label: Insurance Company Address
definition: The mailing address of the Insurance Company.

name: ePayment.13
label: Insurance Company City
definition: The insurance company's city or township used for mailing purposes.
element_comments: The city elements within the ePayment section have been updated to allow free text. This is important for mailing address purposes to submit claims and identify the correct payer.

name: ePayment.14
label: Insurance Company State
definition: The insurance company's state, territory, or province, or District of Columbia.
element_comments: Based on the ANSI State Code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

name: ePayment.15
label: Insurance Company ZIP Code
definition: The insurance company's ZIP Code.
element_comments: ZIP Codes Product Website:  https://www.zipcodedownload.com/Products/Product/Z5Commercial/Standard/Overview/ Product: USA - 5-digit ZIP Code Database, Commercial Edition

name: ePayment.16
label: Insurance Company Country
definition: The insurance company's country.
element_comments: Based on the ISO Country Code. ANSI Country Codes (ISO 3166) Website: https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html
version3_changes: Added to better document international addresses.

name: ePayment.17
label: Insurance Group ID
definition: The ID number of the patient's insurance group.

name: ePayment.18
label: Insurance Policy ID Number
definition: The ID number of the patient's insurance policy.

name: ePayment.19
label: Last Name of the Insured
definition: The last (family) name of the person insured by the insurance company.

name: ePayment.20
label: First Name of the Insured
definition: The first (given) name of the person insured by the insurance company.

name: ePayment.21
label: Middle Initial/Name of the Insured
definition: The middle name, if any, of the person insured by the insurance company.

name: ePayment.22
label: Relationship to the Insured
definition: The relationship of the patient to the primary insured person.
element_comments: Descriptors are consistent with X12 Loop 2000C PAT01, Specifies the patient's relationship to the person insured.

name: ePayment.23
label: Closest Relative/Guardian Last Name
definition: The last (family) name of the patient's closest relative or guardian.

name: ePayment.24
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian First Name
definition: The first (given) name of the patient's closest relative or guardian.

name: ePayment.25
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian Middle Initial/Name
definition: The middle name/initial, if any, of the closest patient's relative or guardian.

name: ePayment.26
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian Street Address
definition: The street address of the residence of the patient's closest relative or guardian.
element_comments: This element allows for a two line documentation of the address. For out of country addresses the second line should be used to document, city, country, postal code and any other pertinent information.

name: ePayment.27
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian City
definition: The primary city or township of residence of the patient's closest relative or guardian.
element_comments: The city elements within the ePayment section have been updated to allow free text. This is important for mailing address purposes to submit claims and identify the correct payer.

name: ePayment.28
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian State
definition: The state of residence of the patient's closest relative or guardian.
element_comments: Based on ANSI State Code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

name: ePayment.29
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian ZIP Code
definition: The ZIP Code of the residence of the patient's closest relative or guardian.
element_comments: ZIP Codes Product Website:  https://www.zipcodedownload.com/Products/Product/Z5Commercial/Standard/Overview/ Product: USA - 5-digit ZIP Code Database, Commercial Edition

name: ePayment.30
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian Country
definition: The country of residence of the patient's closest relative or guardian.
element_comments: Based on the ISO Country Code. ANSI Country Codes (ISO 3166) Website: https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html
version3_changes: Added to improve international compatibility.

name: ePayment.31
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian Phone Number
definition: The phone number of the patient's closest relative or guardian.

name: ePayment.32
label: Closest Relative/ Guardian Relationship
definition: The relationship of the patient's closest relative or guardian.

name: ePayment.33
label: Patient's Employer
definition: The patient's employer's name.

name: ePayment.34
label: Patient's Employer's Address
definition: The street address of the patient's employer.
element_comments: This element allows for a two line documentation of the address. For out of country addresses the second line should be used to document, city, country, postal code and any other pertinent information.

name: ePayment.35
label: Patient's Employer's City
definition: The city or township of the patient's employer used for mailing purposes.
element_comments: The city elements within the ePayment section have been updated to allow free text. This is important for mailing address purposes to submit claims and identify the correct payer.

name: ePayment.36
label: Patient's Employer's State
definition: The state of the patient's employer.
element_comments: Based on ANSI State Code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

name: ePayment.37
label: Patient's Employer's ZIP Code
definition: The ZIP Code of the patient's employer.
element_comments: ZIP Codes Product Website:  https://www.zipcodedownload.com/Products/Product/Z5Commercial/Standard/Overview/ Product: USA - 5-digit ZIP Code Database, Commercial Edition

name: ePayment.38
label: Patient's Employer's Country
definition: The country of the patient's employer.
element_comments: Based on the ISO Country Code. ANSI Country Codes (ISO 3166) Website: https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html
version3_changes: Added to improve international compatibility.

name: ePayment.39
label: Patient's Employer's Primary Phone Number
definition: The employer's primary phone number.

name: ePayment.40
label: Response Urgency
definition: The urgency in which the EMS agency began to mobilize resources for this EMS encounter.
element_comments: This is for billing documentation.

name: ePayment.41
label: Patient Transport Assessment
definition: Documentation of the patient's transport need based on mobility and/or physical capability.
element_comments: Added to better justify the medical necessity of the transport. Based on CMS 42 CFR Ch IV (10-1-02 Edition) & 410.40 (d) medical necessity requirement (1)

name: ePayment.42
label: Specialty Care Transport Care Provider
definition: Documentation to show the patient care provided to the patient met the Specialty Care Transport Base Rate requirements.
element_comments: Added to improve Specialty Care Transport billing justification. In accordance with CMS requirements Specialty Care Transport (SCT): ""Additional training" means the specific additional training that a State requires a paramedic to complete in order to qualify to furnish specialty care to a critically ill or injured patient during an SCT."

name: ePayment.44
label: Ambulance Transport Reason Code
definition: The CMS Ambulance Transport Reason Code for the transport.

allowed_values.display: Patient was transported to the nearest facility for care of symptoms, complaints, or both
Patient was transported for the benefit of a preferred physician
Patient was transported for the nearness of family members
Patient was transported for the care of a specialist or for availability of equipment
Patient was transferred to a Rehabilitation Facility

element_comments: Added to improve billing with electronic claims using the ASC X12 Standard. The Ambulance Transport Code values are those determined by US Dept of HHS for the "Code indicating the type of ambulance transport". The Accredited Standards Committee X12 organization maintains the standard. See the X12 website http://www.x12.org or United States Health Information Knowledgebase website for more information: https://ushik.ahrq.gov/ViewItemDetails?system=sdo&itemKey=133080000

name: ePayment.45
label: Round Trip Purpose Description
definition: Free text description providing the purpose of the round trip EMS transport based on CR109 field for CMS.
element_comments: Added to improve billing documentation for CMS X12 based transactions. Ambulance providers can use this field to provide additional information regarding the circumstances of a round trip transport or for other medical necessity documentation.
version3_changes: Added to improve billing with electronic claims using X12

name: ePayment.46
label: Stretcher Purpose Description
definition: Free Text Documentation providing the reason for use of a stretcher in the EMS patient transport.
element_comments: Added to improve billing using CMS X12 transactions based on CR110. Documentation of Stretcher Purpose Description (ePayment.46) is required when needed to justify use of a stretcher. (X12 - Loop 2300 CR110).

name: ePayment.47
label: Ambulance Conditions Indicator
definition: Documentation of the CRC03 through CRC07 requirements for CMS billing using X12 transactions.

allowed_values.display: Patient was admitted to a hospital
Patient was unconscious or in shock
Patient was transported in an emergency situation
Patient had to be physically restrained
Patient is confined to a bed or chair (Use code 12 to indicate patient was bedridden during transport.)

element_comments: Added to improve billing documentation and justification.
version3_changes: Added to improve billing with electronic claims using X12. The Codes and Descriptors updated Sept 2013 to meet the X12 5010 standard.

name: ePayment.48
label: Mileage to Closest Hospital Facility
definition: The mileage to the closest hospital facility from the scene. Documented only if the patient was transported to a facility farther away than the closest hospital.
element_comments: Added to clarify billing mileage when the patient is not taken to the closest facility.
version3_changes: Added for improved billing documentation.

name: ePayment.49
label: ALS Assessment Performed and Warranted
definition: Documentation that the patient required an ALS assessment and it was performed.
element_comments: Added to improve billing justification
version3_changes: Added to improve billing justification

name: ePayment.50
label: CMS Service Level
definition: The CMS service level for this EMS encounter.

validation_rules.message: When CMS Service Level is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When CMS Service Level has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: ePayment.51
label: EMS Condition Code
definition: The condition code associated with the CMS EMS negotiated rule-making process.
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM Codes: Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

name: ePayment.52
label: CMS Transportation Indicator
definition: The CMS Ambulance Fee Schedule Transportation and Air Medical Transportation Indicators are used to better describe why it was necessary for the patient to be transported in a particular way or circumstance.

allowed_values.display: Interfacility Transport (Requires Higher level of care)
Emergency Trauma Dispatch Condition Code (Major Incident or Mechanism of Injury)
Medically Necessary Transport (Facility on Divert or Services Unavailable)
BLS Transport of ALS Patient (ALS not available)
ALS Response (Based on Dispatch Info) to BLS Patient (Condition)
Long Distance-patient's condition requires rapid transportation over a long distance
Rare Circumstances, Traffic Patterns Precludes Ground Transport
Time to the closest appropriate hospital due to the patient's condition precludes ground transport; maximize clinical benefits
Pick up Point not Accessible by Ground Transport

element_comments: Name changed from Condition Code Modifier to CMS Transportation Indicator
version3_changes: Name changed from Condition Code Modifier to CMS Transportation Indicator

name: ePayment.53
label: Transport Authorization Code
definition: Prior authorization code provided by the insurance carrier/payer.
version3_changes: Added to improve billing documentation.

name: ePayment.54
label: Prior Authorization Code Payer
definition: The Payer who has provided the Prior Authorization Code.
element_comments: Added to document Prior Authorization Codes for an EMS Transport.
version3_changes: Added to improve billing documentation.

name: ePayment.55
label: Supply Item Used Name
definition: The name of the supply used on the patient by the EMS Crew during the EMS event.
element_comments: Added to track EMS supplies for billing. The list of supplies would be created by the EMS Agency. There is no master list of supply items defined.
version3_changes: Added to track EMS supplies for billing.

name: ePayment.56
label: Number of Supply Item(s) Used
definition: The number of the specific supply item used on the patient by the EMS Crew during the EMS event.
element_comments: Associated with ePayment.55 (Supply Item Used Name). Used to better track supply items for billing.
version3_changes: Added to track EMS supplies for billing.

name: ePayment.57
label: Payer Type
definition: Payer type according to X12 standard.
element_comments: This element should only be used if Insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, Workers Compensation, or Other Government are selected in ePayment.01 - Primary Method of Payment. eScene OeScene.23 - Incident Census Tract0 : 1 OeScene.22 - Incident Country0 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.21 - Incident County1 : 1 N, LESeScene.20 - Scene Cross Street or Directions0 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.19 - Incident ZIP Code1 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.18 - Incident State1 : 1 N, LESeScene.17 - Incident City0 : 1 N, LESeScene.16 - Incident Apartment, Suite, or Room0 : 1 N, LESeScene.15 - Incident Street Address0 : 1 N, LESeScene.14 - Mile Post or Major Roadway0 : 1 OSeScene.13 - Incident Facility or Location Name0 : 1 OSeScene.12 - Scene US National Grid Coordinates0 : 1 OSeScene.11 - Scene GPS Location0 : 1 N, LESeScene.10 - Incident Facility Code0 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.09 - Incident Location Type1 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.08 - Triage Classification for MCI Patient1 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.07 - Mass Casualty Incident1 : 1 N, LRSNeScene.06 - Number of Patients at Scene1 : 1 OeScene.05 - Date/Time Initial Responder Arrived on Scene0 : 1 N, LESeScene.25 - Transferred Patient/Care To/From Agency0 : 1 N, LOeScene.24 - First Other EMS or Public Safety Agency at Scene to Provide Patient Care 0 : 1 OeScene.04 - Type of Other Service at Scene0 : 1 OeScene.03 - Other EMS or Public Safety Agency ID Number0 : 1 OeScene.02 - Other EMS or Public Safety Agencies at Scene0 : 1 CeScene.ResponderGroup0 : M N, LRSNeScene.01 - First EMS Unit on Scene1 : 1 eScene eScene

name: ePayment.58
label: Insurance Group Name
definition: The name of the patient's insurance group.

name: ePayment.59
label: Insurance Company Phone Number
definition: The phone number for the patient's insurance company.

name: ePayment.60
label: Date of Birth of the Insured
official_name: Date of Birth of the Insured
definition: The date of birth of the person insured by the insurance company.

name: eProcedures.01
label: Date/Time Procedure Performed
definition: The date/time the procedure was performed on the patient.
element_comments: DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

validation_rules.message: When Date/Time Procedure Performed is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Date/Time Procedure Performed has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should be recorded, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be earlier than Unit Notified by Dispatch Date/Time, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be earlier than Arrived at Patient Date/Time, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Destination Patient Transfer of Care Date/Time.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Unit Back in Service Date/Time.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Arrived at Patient Date/Time when Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".

name: eProcedures.02
label: Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care
official_name: Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care
definition: Indicates that the procedure which was performed and documented was performed prior to this EMS units care.
element_comments: This is the NEMSIS Version 3 method to document prior aid.

validation_rules.message: When Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should be recorded, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be earlier than Unit Notified by Dispatch Date/Time, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be earlier than Arrived at Patient Date/Time, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Destination Patient Transfer of Care Date/Time.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Unit Back in Service Date/Time.
Date/Time Procedure Performed should not be later than Arrived at Patient Date/Time when Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".
Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure should be recorded, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".

name: eProcedures.03
label: Procedure
definition: The procedure performed on the patient.
element_comments: Procedures which are recorded as a Vital Sign do not have to be documented in the Procedure Section. Code list is represented in SNOMEDCT. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ SNOMEDCT Website: http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/Snomed/snomed_main.html Product: Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Procedure is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Procedure has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Procedure has a Pertinent Negative, it should have a value and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
Procedure should be recorded when a procedure is performed.

name: eProcedures.04
label: Size of Procedure Equipment
definition: The size of the equipment used in the procedure on the patient.
element_comments: Need business logic to require detail of size appropriate for the procedure.

name: eProcedures.05
label: Number of Procedure Attempts
definition: The number of attempts taken to complete a procedure or intervention regardless of success.

validation_rules.message: When Number of Procedure Attempts is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Number of Procedure Attempts has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProcedures.06
label: Procedure Successful
definition: Indicates that this individual procedure attempt which was performed on the patient was successful.

validation_rules.message: When Procedure Successful is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Procedure Successful has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProcedures.07
label: Procedure Complication
definition: Any complication (abnormal effect on the patient) associated with the performance of the procedure on the patient.

validation_rules.message: When Procedure Complication is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Procedure Complication has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Procedure Complication has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Procedure Complication is "None", no other value should be recorded.

name: eProcedures.08
label: Response to Procedure
definition: The patient's response to the procedure.

validation_rules.message: When Response to Procedure is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Response to Procedure has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProcedures.09
label: Procedure Crew Members ID
definition: The statewide assigned ID number of the EMS crew member performing the procedure on the patient.
element_comments: For an incident that occurs in multiple states, the certification ID number the EMS agency would typically use is based on the EMS agency's state license specific to each EMS professional. If the incident needs to be reported to each state, then the EMS Agency Number for each state should be submitted as well as the certification ID numbers for each EMS professional on the unit.

validation_rules.message: When Procedure Crew Members ID is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Procedure Crew Members ID has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProcedures.10
label: Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure
official_name: Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure
definition: The type (level) of EMS or Healthcare Professional performing the procedure. For procedures performed prior to EMS arrival, this may be a non-EMS healthcare professional.
element_comments: Added to document the type of healthcare professional administering the medication. This could be auto-completed from the crew ID but is necessary to document medication administration prior to EMS arrival. State may maintain an enumerated list but must collapse to the National Standard. The category EMT-Intermediate includes EMS professionals with an "85" or "99" certification level.

validation_rules.message: When Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Role/Type of Person Performing the Procedure should be recorded, unless Procedure Performed Prior to this Unit's EMS Care is "Yes".

version3_changes: Added to document the type of healthcare professional performing the procedure.

name: eProcedures.11
label: Procedure Authorization
definition: The type of treatment authorization obtained.

name: eProcedures.12
label: Procedure Authorizing Physician
definition: The name of the authorizing physician ordering the procedure, if the order was provided by any manner other than protocol (standing order) in eProcedures.11.

name: eProcedures.13
label: Vascular Access Location
definition: The location of the vascular access site attempt on the patient, if applicable.
element_comments: This is now associated with the Date/Time of the procedure and therefore changed to single choice. This allows the location to be documented with each procedure and attempt. If the vascular access has been established prior to EMS, this should be documented as such.

validation_rules.message: When Vascular Access Location is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Vascular Access Location has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProcedures.14
label: Airway Placement Technique
definition: The manner/equipment used during the placement of the advanced airway.

validation_rules.message: When Airway Placement Technique is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Airway Placement Technique has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Airway Placement Technique has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.

version3_changes: Added to better document airway management.

name: eProcedures.15
label: Procedure Performed by Another Unit
definition: Indicates the procedure was performed by someone other than the EMS clinicians in this EMS unit. This is not for prior to arrival procedures, but for procedures performed when multiple units/caregivers are on scene simultaneously providing patient care.
element_comments: This is the method to document when care is provided by others when multiple units are present at the scene.

validation_rules.message: When Procedure Performed by Another Unit is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).

When Procedure Performed by Another Unit has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty. eAirway OeAirway.11 - Date/Time Invasive Airway Placement Attempts Abandoned0 : 1 OeAirway.10 - Date/Time Decision to Manage the Patient with an Invasive Airway0 : 1 COSeAirway.09 - Suspected Reasons for Failed Airway Management0 : M CN, L, PESeAirway.08 - Airway Complications Encountered0 : M N, LESeAirway.07 - Crew Member ID0 : 1 N, LESeAirway.06 - Type of Individual Confirming Airway Device Placement0 : 1 OeAirway.05 - Tube Depth0 : 1 CN, LESeAirway.04 - Airway Device Placement Confirmed Method0 : M N, LESeAirway.03 - Airway Device Being Confirmed0 : 1 N, LESeAirway.02 - Date/Time Airway Device Placement Confirmation0 : 1 GCeAirway.ConfirmationGroup0 : M CN, LESeAirway.01 - Indications for Invasive Airway0 : M eAirway.AirwayGroup0 : 1 eAirway eAirway

name: eProtocols.01
label: Protocols Used
definition: The protocol used by EMS personnel to direct the clinical care of the patient.

allowed_values.display: Cardiac Arrest-Ventricular Fibrillation/ Pulseless Ventricular Tachycardia
General-Universal Patient Care/ Initial Patient Contact
General-Law Enforcement - Blood for Legal Purposes
General-Law Enforcement - Assist with Law Enforcement Activity
Cardiac Arrest-Determination of Death / Withholding Resuscitative Efforts

element_comments: Protocols are grouped into Airway, Cardiac, Environmental, Exposure, General, Injury, Medical, and OB/GYN. There is a new data element eProtocols.02 to indicate whether the protocol is Adult, Pediatric, or General (both). State and local entities can add additional protocols to the list but the additional protocols must map to these uniform codes. The protocol code list should be derived from dConfiguration.05 and/or dConfiguration.10

validation_rules.message: When Protocols Used is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Protocols Used has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eProtocols.02
label: Protocol Age Category
definition: The age group the protocol is written to address.
element_comments: Protocols are grouped into Airway, Environmental, Exposure, General, Injury, Medical, and OB/GYN. -There is a new data element to indicate whether the protocol is Adult, Pediatric, or General (both).

validation_rules.message: When Protocol Age Category is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).

When Protocol Age Category has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty. eMedications N, LESeMedications.13 - Medication Administered by Another Unit0 : 1 OeMedications.12 - Medication Authorizing Physician0 : 1 OeMedications.11 - Medication Authorization0 : 1 N, LRSNeMedications.10 - Role/Type of Person Administering Medication1 : 1 N, LESeMedications.09 - Medication Crew (Healthcare Professionals) ID0 : 1 CN, LRSNeMedications.08 - Medication Complication1 : M N, LRSNeMedications.07 - Response to Medication1 : 1 N, LRSNeMedications.06 - Medication Dosage Units1 : 1 N, LRSNeMedications.05 - Medication Dosage1 : 1 eMedications.DosageGroup1 : 1 N, L, PRSNeMedications.04 - Medication Administered Route1 : 1 N, L, PRSNeMedications.03 - Medication Administered1 : 1 N, LRSNeMedications.02 - Medication Administered Prior to this Unit's EMS Care1 : 1 N, LRSNeMedications.01 - Date/Time Medication Administered1 : 1 CeMedications.MedicationGroup1 : M eMedications eMedications

version3_changes: Added to better document protocol use.

name: eRecord.01
label: Patient Care Report Number
definition: The unique number automatically assigned by the EMS agency for each Patient Care Report (PCR). This should be a unique number for the EMS agency for all of time.

name: eRecord.02
label: Software Creator
definition: The name of the vendor, manufacturer, and developer who designed the application that created this record.
element_comments: This is required to document the software used to generate the Patient Care Report. This is not the last software which aggregated/stored the Patient Care Report after it was sent from another software.

name: eRecord.03
label: Software Name
definition: The name of the application used to create this record.
element_comments: This is the EMS Agency's software, not the state or other level software which electronically received the data from the local EMS Agency.

name: eRecord.04
label: Software Version
definition: The version of the application used to create this record.
element_comments: eResponse CN, LRSNeResponse.24 - Additional Response Mode Descriptors1 : M MSNeResponse.23 - Response Mode to Scene1 : 1 OSeResponse.22 - Ending Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle0 : 1 OSeResponse.21 - Patient Destination Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle0 : 1 OSeResponse.20 - On-Scene Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle0 : 1 OSeResponse.19 - Beginning Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle0 : 1 OeResponse.18 - Vehicle Dispatch Location US National Grid Coordinates0 : 1 OeResponse.17 - Vehicle Dispatch GPS Location0 : 1 OeResponse.16 - Vehicle Dispatch Location0 : 1 MSNeResponse.14 - EMS Unit Call Sign1 : 1 MSNeResponse.13 - EMS Vehicle (Unit) Number1 : 1 CN, LRSNeResponse.12 - Type of Turn-Around Delay1 : M CN, LRSNeResponse.11 - Type of Transport Delay1 : M CN, LRSNeResponse.10 - Type of Scene Delay1 : M CN, LRSNeResponse.09 - Type of Response Delay1 : M CN, LRSNeResponse.08 - Type of Dispatch Delay1 : M MSNeResponse.07 - Unit Transport and Equipment Capability1 : 1 OeResponse.06 - Standby Purpose0 : 1 MSNeResponse.05 - Type of Service Requested1 : 1 eResponse.ServiceGroup1 : 1 N, LRSNeResponse.04 - EMS Response Number1 : 1 N, LRSNeResponse.03 - Incident Number1 : 1 N, LESeResponse.02 - EMS Agency Name0 : 1 MSNeResponse.01 - EMS Agency Number1 : 1 eResponse.AgencyGroup1 : 1 eResponse eResponse

name: eResponse.01
label: EMS Agency Number
definition: The state-assigned provider number of the responding agency.
element_comments: The EMS Agency Number in eResponse.01 can auto-populate from dAgency.02 EMS Agency Number in the demographic section.

validation_rules.message: EMS Agency Number in the patient care report should match EMS Agency Number in the agency demographic information.

name: eResponse.02
label: EMS Agency Name
definition: The EMS agency's name.
element_comments: Added to better identify the EMS Agency associated with the EMS event.

validation_rules.message: When EMS Agency Name is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When EMS Agency Name has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eResponse.03
label: Incident Number
definition: The incident number assigned by the 911 Dispatch System.
element_comments: This number can be used to associate multiple EMS responses, dispatch information, and other information to the same EMS event or patient.

validation_rules.message: When Incident Number is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident Number has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eResponse.04
label: EMS Response Number
definition: The internal EMS response number which is unique for each EMS Vehicle's (Unit) response to an incident within an EMS Agency.

validation_rules.message: When EMS Response Number is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When EMS Response Number has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eResponse.05
label: Type of Service Requested
definition: The type of service or category of service requested of the EMS Agency responding for this specific EMS event.

allowed_values.display: Non-Hospital Facility to Non-Hospital Facility Transfer
Transport of Organs or Body Parts

element_comments: Values for "911 Response (Scene)", "Intercept", and "Mutual Aid" have been relabeled to start with "Emergency Response" to more easily identify these options. "Interfacility Transport" was relabeled to "Hospital-to-Hospital Transfer" to be more accurate; "Medical Transport" was relabeled to "Other Routine Medical Transport" to cover any other medical transports such as transports to and from dialysis, doctor appointments, return home, or nursing homes. Values added to consolidate types of service previously captured in "Primary Role of Unit" and eDisposition.12 as found in V3.4.0. Additional values added to reflect emerging service types.

validation_rules.message: Date/Time of Symptom Onset should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Possible Injury should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Primary Symptom should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Provider's Primary Impression should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Initial Patient Acuity should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport should only be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "... Transfer" or "Other Routine Medical Transport".

version3_changes: With the release of v3.5.0 values were added to meet the needs of EMS

name: eResponse.06
label: Standby Purpose
definition: The main reason the EMS Unit is on Standby as the Primary Type of Service for the EMS event.
element_comments: Added to document the reason for "Standby" when populated in eResponse.05 (Type of Service Requested). This information will assist in administrative analysis of EMS service delivery, special event coverage, etc.

name: eResponse.07
label: Unit Transport and Equipment Capability
definition: The transport and equipment capabilities of the EMS Unit which responded to this specific EMS event.
element_comments: Element relabeled from "Primary Role of Unit" to "Unit Transport and Equipment Capability" to better reflect its new defined purpose and allow the deprecation of eResponse.15 "Level of Care of This Unit". V3.4.0 Non-Transport values deprecated as these are types of service and were moved to eResponse.05 "Type of Service Requested". Values have been added to better capture transport capability and available equipment. These values should be tied to the capabilities and role of the unit and not reflect the level of providers responding to an event. Supervisor clarified to Administrative Only. If the Supervisor is responding to assist, that would be considered Non-Transport. Transport separated into Air and Ground. In Version 2 there was no way to identify Air Transport.
version3_changes: With the release of V3.5.0 values were deprecated, added, and relabeled to meet the needs of EMS.

name: eResponse.08
label: Type of Dispatch Delay
definition: The dispatch delays, if any, associated with the dispatch of the EMS unit to the EMS event.
element_comments: A dispatch delay is any time delay that occurs from the time of PSAP call (eTimes.01) to the time the unit is notified by dispatch (eTimes.03).

validation_rules.message: When Type of Dispatch Delay is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Type of Dispatch Delay has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Type of Dispatch Delay has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Type of Dispatch Delay is "None/No Delay", no other value should be recorded.

name: eResponse.09
label: Type of Response Delay
definition: The response delays, if any, of the EMS unit associated with the EMS event.
element_comments: Rendezvous Transport Unavailable added for situations where there is a wait for an EMS Transport Unit, a Ferry, Air Medical, etc. to return to service. A response delay is any time delay that occurs from the time the unit is notified by dispatch (eTimes.03) to the time the unit arrived on scene (eTimes.06).

validation_rules.message: When Type of Response Delay is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Type of Response Delay has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Type of Response Delay has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Type of Response Delay is "None/No Delay", no other value should be recorded.

name: eResponse.10
label: Type of Scene Delay
definition: The scene delays, if any, of the EMS unit associated with the EMS event.
element_comments: A scene delay is any time delay that occurs from the time the unit arrived on scene (eTimes.06) to the time the unit left the scene (eTimes.09).

validation_rules.message: When Type of Scene Delay is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Type of Scene Delay has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Type of Scene Delay has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Type of Scene Delay is "None/No Delay", no other value should be recorded.

name: eResponse.11
label: Type of Transport Delay
definition: The transport delays, if any, of the EMS unit associated with the EMS event.
element_comments: Rendezvous Transport Unavailable added for situations where there is a wait for an EMS Transport Unit, a Ferry, Air Medical, etc. A transport delay is any time delay that occurs from the time the unit left the scene (eTimes.09) to the time the patient arrived at the destination (eTimes.10).

validation_rules.message: When Type of Transport Delay is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Type of Transport Delay has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Type of Transport Delay has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Type of Transport Delay is "None/No Delay", no other value should be recorded.
