name: eResponse.12
label: Type of Turn-Around Delay
definition: The turn-around delays, if any, of EMS unit associated with the EMS event.

allowed_values.display: EMS Crew Accompanies Patient for Facility Procedure

element_comments: Rendezvous Transport Unavailable added for situations where there is a wait for an EMS Transport Unit, a Ferry, Air Medical, etc. to return to service. If a patient is being transported by the unit, turn-around delay is any time delay that occurs from the time the patient arrived at the destination (eTimes.11) until the time the unit is back in service (eTimes.13) or unit back at the home location (eTimes.15) [whichever is the greater of the two times]. If no patient is being transported by the unit, turn-around delay is any time delay that occurs from the time the unit arrived on scene (eTimes.06) until the unit is back in service (eTimes.13) or the unit back at the home location (eTimes.15) [whichever is the greater of the two times].

validation_rules.message: When Type of Turn-Around Delay is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Type of Turn-Around Delay has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Type of Turn-Around Delay has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
When Type of Turn-Around Delay is "None/No Delay", no other value should be recorded.

name: eResponse.13
label: EMS Vehicle (Unit) Number
definition: The unique physical vehicle number of the responding unit.
element_comments: This is recommended to be the State Vehicle Permit Number if unique to the vehicle. If the vehicle is not licensed by the state, this should be a unique number only associated with a specific vehicle. This element should be populated from dVehicle.01 - Unit/Vehicle Number.

name: eResponse.14
label: EMS Unit Call Sign
definition: The EMS unit number used to dispatch and communicate with the unit. This may be the same as the EMS Unit/Vehicle Number in many agencies.
element_comments: "This element could be populated from a list created in dVehicle.03 EMS Unit Call Sign or dConfiguration.16 (Crew Call Sign).

name: eResponse.16
label: Vehicle Dispatch Location
definition: The geographic location of the EMS unit or crew at the time of dispatch.
element_comments: This element can be populated from dLocation.02 EMS Location Name or dFacility information. Depending on the information the EMS Agency or State is interested in knowing the following elements can be utilized: 1. dLocation.02 EMS Location Name 2. dFacility.02 Facility Name 3. dFacility.03 Facility Code

name: eResponse.17
label: Vehicle Dispatch GPS Location
definition: The GPS coordinates associated with the EMS unit at the time of dispatch documented in decimal degrees.
element_comments: The pattern for GPS location is in the format " , " where: latitude longitude -  has a minimum of -90 and a maximum of 90 with up to 6 decimal places latitude -  has a minimum of -180 and a maximum of 180 with up to 6 decimal placeslongitude

name: eResponse.18
label: Vehicle Dispatch Location US National Grid Coordinates
official_name: Vehicle Dispatch Location US National Grid Coordinates
definition: The US National Grid Coordinates for the EMS Vehicle's Dispatch Location.
element_comments: Standard found at www.fgdc.gov/usng. Used by the EMS components of US Governmental entities such as national parks and military agencies.

name: eResponse.19
label: Beginning Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
official_name: Beginning Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
definition: The mileage (counter or odometer reading) of the vehicle at the beginning of the call (when the wheels begin moving). If EMS vehicle/unit is via water or air travel document the number in "hours" as it relates to the documentation of Boat, Fixed Wing, or Rotor Craft in eDisposition.16 (EMS Transport Method).
element_comments: If a mileage counter is being used instead of an odometer, this value would be 0.

name: eResponse.20
label: On-Scene Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
official_name: On-Scene Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
definition: The mileage (counter or odometer reading) of the vehicle when it arrives at the scene. If EMS vehicle/unit is via water or air travel document the number in "hours" as it relates to the documentation of Boat, Fixed Wing, or Rotor Craft in eDisposition.16 (EMS Transport Method).
element_comments: If using a counter, this is the mileage traveled from dispatch to the scene starting from 0.

name: eResponse.21
label: Patient Destination Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
official_name: Patient Destination Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
definition: The mileage (counter or odometer reading) of the vehicle when it arrives at the patient's destination. If EMS vehicle/unit is via water or air travel document the number in "hours" as it relates to the documentation of Boat, Fixed Wing, or Rotor Craft in eDisposition.16 (EMS Transport Method).
element_comments: If using a counter, this is the mileage traveled from dispatch to the patient's transport destination starting from 0.

name: eResponse.22
label: Ending Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
official_name: Ending Odometer Reading of Responding Vehicle
definition: If using a counter, this is the mileage traveled beginning with dispatch through the transport of the patient to their destination and ending when back in service, starting from 0. If EMS vehicle/unit is via water or air travel document the number in "hours" as it relates to the documentation of boat, Fixed Wing, or Rotor Craft in eDisposition.16.

name: eResponse.23
label: Response Mode to Scene
definition: The indication whether the response was emergent or non-emergent. An emergent response is an immediate response.
element_comments: Information now split between eResponse.23 (Response Mode to Scene) and eResponse.24 (Additional Response Mode Descriptors)

name: eResponse.24
label: Additional Response Mode Descriptors
definition: The documentation of response mode techniques used for this EMS response.

allowed_values.display: Intersection Navigation-With Automated Light Changing Technology
Initial No Lights or Sirens, Upgraded to Lights and Sirens
Initial Lights and Sirens, Downgraded to No Lights or Sirens

element_comments: Descriptors have been added to better describe the EMS Response. This includes information on whether the EMS event was schedule or unscheduled.

validation_rules.message: When Additional Response Mode Descriptors is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Additional Response Mode Descriptors has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

When Additional Response Mode Descriptors has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded. eDispatch OeDispatch.06 - Unit Dispatched CAD Record ID0 : 1 OeDispatch.05 - Dispatch Priority (Patient Acuity)0 : 1 OeDispatch.04 - Dispatch Center Name or ID0 : 1 OeDispatch.03 - EMD Determinant Code0 : 1 N, LRSNeDispatch.02 - EMD Performed1 : 1 MSNeDispatch.01 - Dispatch Reason1 : 1 eDispatch eDispatch

name: eScene.01
label: First EMS Unit on Scene
definition: Documentation that this EMS Unit was the first EMS Unit among all EMS Agencies on the Scene.
element_comments: Added to improve the evaluation of Response Times when multiple EMS units are responding to the same scene.

validation_rules.message: When First EMS Unit on Scene is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When First EMS Unit on Scene has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.02
label: Other EMS or Public Safety Agencies at Scene
official_name: Other EMS or Public Safety Agencies at Scene
definition: The name(s) of other EMS or public safety agencies at the scene.
element_comments: For states and local agencies that participate in the Cardiac Arrest Registry for Enhanced Survival (CARES), eScene.02 and eScene.03 are used to populate CARES data element #16 Fire/First Responder as defined by CARES in the CARES Data Dictionary: .https://vendors.mycares.net/

name: eScene.03
label: Other EMS or Public Safety Agency ID Number
official_name: Other EMS or Public Safety Agency ID Number
definition: The ID number for the EMS Agency or Other Public Safety listed in eScene.02.
element_comments: The ID should be the state ID (license number) for the EMS agency or ID number for the law enforcement. This may be an ID assigned by the state or created by the EMS agency. For states and local agencies that participate in the Cardiac Arrest Registry for Enhanced Survival (CARES), eScene.02 and eScene.03 are used to populate CARES data element #16 Fire/First Responder as defined by CARES in the CARES Data Dictionary: .https://vendors.mycares.net/

name: eScene.04
label: Type of Other Service at Scene
official_name: Type of Other Service at Scene
definition: The type of public safety or EMS service associated with Other Agencies on Scene.
element_comments: Associated with each Other Service in eScene.02

name: eScene.05
label: Date/Time Initial Responder Arrived on Scene
official_name: Date/Time Initial Responder Arrived on Scene
definition: The time that the initial responder arrived on the scene, if applicable.
element_comments: DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

name: eScene.06
label: Number of Patients at Scene
definition: Indicator of how many total patients were at the scene.

validation_rules.message: When Number of Patients at Scene is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Number of Patients at Scene has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Number of Patients at Scene should be "Multiple" or "Single" when Unit Disposition is "Patient Contact Made".
Number of Patients at Scene should be "Multiple" when Mass Casualty Incident is "Yes".

name: eScene.07
label: Mass Casualty Incident
definition: Indicator if this event would be considered a mass casualty incident (overwhelmed existing EMS resources).

validation_rules.message: When Mass Casualty Incident is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Mass Casualty Incident has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Number of Patients at Scene should be "Multiple" when Mass Casualty Incident is "Yes".
Triage Classification for MCI Patient should be recorded when Mass Casualty Incident is "Yes".

name: eScene.08
label: Triage Classification for MCI Patient
definition: The color associated with the initial triage assessment/classification of the MCI patient.
element_comments: This element is documented when eScene.07 (Mass Casualty Incident) = Yes. Examples of triage systems include START and SALT. Adapted from: SALT mass casualty triage: concept endorsed by the American College of Emergency Physicians, American College of Surgeons Committee on Trauma, American Trauma Society, National Association of EMS Physicians, National Disaster Life Support Education Consortium, and State and Territorial Injury Prevention Directors Association. Disaster Med Public Health Prep. 2008 Dec;2(4):245-6. START reference: Benson M, Koenig KL, Schultz CH. Disaster triage: START, then SAVE-a new method of dynamic triage for victims of a catastrophic earthquake. Prehospital Disaster Med. 1996; Apr-Jun; 11(2): 117-24

validation_rules.message: When Triage Classification for MCI Patient is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Triage Classification for MCI Patient has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Triage Classification for MCI Patient should be recorded when Mass Casualty Incident is "Yes".

name: eScene.09
label: Incident Location Type
definition: The kind of location where the incident happened.
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ ICD-10-CM Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Incident Location Type is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident Location Type has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Incident Location Type should be recorded unless Unit Disposition is "Cancelled Prior to Arrival at Scene".

name: eScene.10
label: Incident Facility Code
definition: The state, regulatory, or other unique number (code) associated with the facility if the Incident is a Healthcare Facility.
element_comments: This may be populated from a list created within dFacility.03 (Facility Location Code) or dFacility.05 (Facility National Provider Identifier).

validation_rules.message: When Incident Facility Code is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident Facility Code has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.11
label: Scene GPS Location
definition: The GPS coordinates associated with the Scene.
element_comments: The pattern for GPS location is in the format " , " where: latitude longitude -  has a minimum of -90 and a maximum of 90 with up to 6 decimal places latitude -  has a minimum of -180 and a maximum of 180 with up to 6 decimal placeslongitude

name: eScene.12
label: Scene US National Grid Coordinates
definition: The US National Grid Coordinates for the Scene.
element_comments: Standard found at www.fgdc.gov/usng

name: eScene.13
label: Incident Facility or Location Name
definition: The name of the facility, business, building, etc. associated with the scene of the EMS event.
element_comments: This element may be populated from a list created within dFacility.02 (Facility Name).

name: eScene.14
label: Mile Post or Major Roadway
definition: The mile post or major roadway associated with the incident locations.

validation_rules.message: When Mile Post or Major Roadway is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Mile Post or Major Roadway has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.15
label: Incident Street Address
definition: The street address where the patient was found, or, if no patient, the address to which the unit responded.

validation_rules.message: When Incident Street Address is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident Street Address has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.16
label: Incident Apartment, Suite, or Room
definition: The number of the specific apartment, suite, or room where the incident occurred.

validation_rules.message: When Incident Apartment, Suite, or Room is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident Apartment, Suite, or Room has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.17
label: Incident City
definition: The city or township (if applicable) where the patient was found or to which the unit responded (or best approximation).
element_comments: City codes are based on GNIS Feature Class. The primary Feature Class to use is "Civil" with "Populated Place" and "Military" code as additional options. Definitions for each GNIS City Feature Class can be found on the GNIS Codes website. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Incident City is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident City has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.18
label: Incident State
definition: The state, territory, or province where the patient was found or to which the unit responded (or best approximation).
element_comments: Based on the ANSI State Code. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Incident State is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident State has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Incident State should be recorded unless Unit Disposition is "Cancelled Prior to Arrival at Scene".
Incident County should belong within the Incident State.

name: eScene.19
label: Incident ZIP Code
definition: The ZIP code of the incident location.
element_comments: ZIP Codes Product Website:  https://www.zipcodedownload.com/Products/Product/Z5Commercial/Standard/Overview/ Product: USA - 5-digit ZIP Code Database, Commercial Edition

validation_rules.message: When Incident ZIP Code is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident ZIP Code has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Incident ZIP Code should be recorded unless Unit Disposition is "Cancelled Prior to Arrival at Scene".

name: eScene.20
label: Scene Cross Street or Directions
definition: The nearest cross street to the incident address or directions from a recognized landmark or the second street name of an intersection.

validation_rules.message: When Scene Cross Street or Directions is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Scene Cross Street or Directions has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

version3_changes: Added to better locate/document the scene (incident) location.

name: eScene.21
label: Incident County
definition: The county or parish where the patient was found or to which the unit responded (or best approximation).
element_comments: Based on the ANSI Code Single Choice based on the County Name but stored as the ANSI code (combined 5 digit State and County codes) Should be required if there is a patient associated with the event. GNIS Codes Website: http://geonames.usgs.gov/domestic/download_data.htm

validation_rules.message: When Incident County is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Incident County has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Incident County should be recorded unless Unit Disposition is "Cancelled Prior to Arrival at Scene".
Incident County should belong within the Incident State.

name: eScene.22
label: Incident Country
definition: The country of the incident location.
element_comments: Based on the ISO Country Code. ANSI Country Codes (ISO 3166) Website: https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html
version3_changes: Added for international compatibility.

name: eScene.23
label: Incident Census Tract
definition: The census tract in which the incident occurred.
element_comments: This data element was added to better document the service area of the EMS Agency. Each state listed is associated with the counties, census tracts, and ZIP codes within the EMS Agency Service Area for each state. The format of the census tract number must be an 11-digit number, based upon the 2010 census, using the pattern: 2-digit State Code 3-digit County Code 6-digit Census Tract Number (no decimal) Example: NEMSIS TAC office (UT, Salt Lake County, Census Tract - located at 295 Chipeta Way, Salt Lake City, UT) 49035101400 Census Tract Data Website (files and descriptions): https://www.census.gov/geographies/reference-files/2010/geo/relationship-files.html
version3_changes: Added to improve the ability to use census and other demographic information within EMS research. eSituation N, LRSNeSituation.20 - Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport1 : 1 N, LESeSituation.19 - Justification for Transfer or Encounter0 : 1 N, L, PRSNeSituation.18 - Date/Time Last Known Well1 : 1 CN, LESeSituation.17 - Patient Activity0 : M OeSituation.16 - Patient's Occupation0 : 1 OeSituation.15 - Patient's Occupational Industry0 : 1 N, LESeSituation.14 - Work-Related Illness/Injury0 : 1 eSituation.WorkRelatedGroup0 : 1 N, LRSNeSituation.13 - Initial Patient Acuity1 : 1 CN, LRSNeSituation.12 - Provider's Secondary Impressions1 : M N, LRSNeSituation.11 - Provider's Primary Impression1 : 1 CN, L, PRSNeSituation.10 - Other Associated Symptoms1 : M N, LRSNeSituation.09 - Primary Symptom1 : 1 N, LRSNeSituation.08 - Chief Complaint Organ System1 : 1 N, LRSNeSituation.07 - Chief Complaint Anatomic Location1 : 1 N, LESeSituation.06 - Time Units of Duration of Complaint0 : 1 N, LESeSituation.05 - Duration of Complaint0 : 1 N, LESeSituation.04 - Complaint0 : 1 N, LESeSituation.03 - Complaint Type0 : 1 CeSituation.PatientComplaintGroup0 : M N, LRSNeSituation.02 - Possible Injury1 : 1 N, L, PRSNeSituation.01 - Date/Time of Symptom Onset1 : 1 eSituation eSituation

name: eScene.24
label: First Other EMS or Public Safety Agency at Scene to Provide Patient Care
official_name: First Other EMS or Public Safety Agency at Scene to Provide Patient Care
definition: When there are multiple other EMS Agencies or Public Safety Agencies at the scene, this element documents the other EMS or public safety agency that was first to provide care to the patient.
element_comments: For states and local agencies that participate in the Cardiac Arrest Registry for Enhanced Survival (CARES), eScene.24 is used to populate CARES data element #16 Fire/First Responder as defined by CARES in the CARES Data Dictionary: .https://vendors.mycares.net/

validation_rules.message: When First Other EMS or Public Safety Agency at Scene to Provide Patient Care is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When First Other EMS or Public Safety Agency at Scene to Provide Patient Care has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eScene.25
label: Transferred Patient/Care To/From Agency
definition: Whether primary responsibility for the care of the patient was transferred in the field to or from the EMS agency listed in eScene.02.

allowed_values.display: No Transfer of Patient/Care To/From Agency

validation_rules.message: When Transferred Patient/Care To/From Agency is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Transferred Patient/Care To/From Agency has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.01
label: Date/Time of Symptom Onset
definition: The date and time the symptom began (or was discovered) as it relates to this EMS event. This is described or estimated by the patient, family, and/or healthcare professionals.
element_comments: DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

validation_rules.message: When Date/Time of Symptom Onset is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Date/Time of Symptom Onset has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Date/Time of Symptom Onset has a Pertinent Negative of "Unable to Complete", it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
When Date/Time of Symptom Onset has a Pertinent Negative of "Approximate", it should have a value and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
Destination Patient Transfer of Care Date/Time should not be earlier than Date/Time of Symptom Onset.
Unit Back in Service Date/Time should not be earlier than Date/Time of Symptom Onset.
Date/Time of Symptom Onset should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".

version3_changes: Added to better define the EMS patient event.

name: eSituation.02
label: Possible Injury
definition: Indication whether or not there was an injury.
element_comments: This data element provides documentation to classify the EMS Reason for Encounter as either injury or non-injury related based on mechanism and not on actual injury. eSituation.02 (Possible Injury), eSituation.09 (Primary Symptom), eSituation.07 (Chief Complaint Anatomic Location), and eSituation.08 (Chief Complaint Organ System) are grouped together to form the EMS Reason for Encounter.

validation_rules.message: When Possible Injury is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Possible Injury has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Possible Injury should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Possible Injury should be "Yes" when a symptom or impression is injury-related.
Cause of Injury should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided" and Possible Injury is "Yes".
Cause of Injury should only be recorded when Possible Injury is "Yes".
Trauma Triage Criteria (High Risk for Serious Injury) should only be recorded when Possible Injury is "Yes".
Trauma Triage Criteria (Moderate Risk for Serious Injury) should only be recorded when Possible Injury is "Yes".

name: eSituation.03
label: Complaint Type
definition: The type of patient healthcare complaint being documented.
element_comments: This was added to group complaints in a more efficient manner

validation_rules.message: When Complaint Type is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Complaint Type has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

version3_changes: Added to improve data integrity.

name: eSituation.04
label: Complaint
definition: The statement of the problem by the patient or the history provider.

validation_rules.message: When Complaint is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Complaint has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.05
label: Duration of Complaint
definition: The duration of the complaint.
element_comments: Associated with eSituation.06 (Time Units of Duration of the Complaint).

validation_rules.message: When Duration of Complaint is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Duration of Complaint has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.06
label: Time Units of Duration of Complaint
official_name: Time Units of Duration of Complaint
definition: The time units of the duration of the patient's complaint.
element_comments: Associated with eSituation.05 (Duration of the Complaint).

validation_rules.message: When Time Units of Duration of Complaint is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Time Units of Duration of Complaint has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.07
label: Chief Complaint Anatomic Location
definition: The primary anatomic location of the chief complaint as identified by EMS personnel.
element_comments: eSituation.02 (Possible Injury), eSituation.09 (Primary Symptom), eSituation.07 (Chief Complaint Anatomic Location), and eSituation.08 (Chief Complaint Organ System) are grouped together to form the EMS Reason for Encounter.

validation_rules.message: When Chief Complaint Anatomic Location is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Chief Complaint Anatomic Location has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.08
label: Chief Complaint Organ System
definition: The primary organ system of the patient injured or medically affected.
element_comments: Altered to follow the anatomical organ systems as defined by general anatomy. Added Lymphatic/Immune; Merged Skin with Muscular-Skeletal. Changed OB/Gyn to Reproductive. Retained non-organ system designations for Global/General and Behavioral/Psychiatric. eSituation.02 (Possible Injury), eSituation.09 (Primary Symptom), eSituation.07 (Chief Complaint Anatomic Location), and eSituation.08 (Chief Complaint Organ System) are grouped together to form the EMS Reason for Encounter.

validation_rules.message: When Chief Complaint Organ System is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Chief Complaint Organ System has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.09
label: Primary Symptom
definition: The primary sign and symptom present in the patient or observed by EMS personnel.
element_comments: eSituation.02 (Possible Injury), eSituation.09 (Primary Symptom), eSituation.07 (Chief Complaint Anatomic Location), and eSituation.08 (Chief Complaint Organ System) are grouped together to form the EMS Reason for Encounter. Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM Diagnosis Codes. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ ICD-10-CM Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Primary Symptom is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Primary Symptom has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Possible Injury should be "Yes" when a symptom or impression is injury-related.
Primary Symptom should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Other Associated Symptoms should only be recorded when Primary Symptom is recorded.

name: eSituation.10
label: Other Associated Symptoms
definition: Other symptoms identified by the patient or observed by EMS personnel.
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ ICD-10-CM Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Other Associated Symptoms is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Other Associated Symptoms has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Other Associated Symptoms has a Pertinent Negative, it should have a value and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
When Other Associated Symptoms has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
Possible Injury should be "Yes" when a symptom or impression is injury-related.
Other Associated Symptoms should only be recorded when Primary Symptom is recorded.

name: eSituation.11
label: Provider's Primary Impression
definition: The EMS personnel's impression of the patient's primary problem or most significant condition which led to the management given to the patient (treatments, medications, or procedures).
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ ICD-10-CM Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Provider's Primary Impression is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Provider's Primary Impression has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Possible Injury should be "Yes" when a symptom or impression is injury-related.
Provider's Primary Impression should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".
Provider's Secondary Impressions should only be recorded when Provider's Primary Impression is recorded.

name: eSituation.12
label: Provider's Secondary Impressions
definition: The EMS personnel's impression of the patient's secondary problem or most significant condition which led to the management given to the patient (treatments, medications, or procedures).
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ ICD-10-CM Website -  http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Provider's Secondary Impressions is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Provider's Secondary Impressions has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Provider's Secondary Impressions has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.
Possible Injury should be "Yes" when a symptom or impression is injury-related.
Provider's Secondary Impressions should only be recorded when Provider's Primary Impression is recorded.

name: eSituation.13
label: Initial Patient Acuity
definition: The acuity of the patient's condition upon this EMS unit's arrival at scene.
element_comments: Definitions related to "Critical, Emergent, and Lower Acuity" can be found in the National EMS Core Content document from NHTSA EMS (DOT HS 809-898 July 2005) at https://www.ems.gov/assets/National_EMS_Core_Content.pdf Dead without Resuscitation Efforts would be appropriate if resuscitation was initiated by non-EMS personnel but discontinued immediately upon evaluation by first arriving EMS personnel. "Non-Acute/Routine" added for use with patients with no clinical issues-such as refusal for a lift assist-or for routine transfers.

validation_rules.message: When Initial Patient Acuity is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Initial Patient Acuity has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Initial Patient Acuity should be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "Emergency Response (Primary Response Area)" and Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided".

version3_changes: With the release of V3.5.0 additional values were added to meet the needs of EMS.

name: eSituation.14
label: Work-Related Illness/Injury
definition: Indication of whether or not the illness or injury is work related.
element_comments: Moved from Version 2.2.1 E07_15 to allow more complete documentation of work related illness and injury.

validation_rules.message: When Work-Related Illness/Injury is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Work-Related Illness/Injury has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

name: eSituation.15
label: Patient's Occupational Industry
definition: The occupational industry of the patient's work.

allowed_values.display: Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services
Mining, Quarrying, and Oil and Gas Extraction
Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

element_comments: From the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) from US Bureau of Labor Statistics (https://www.census.gov/naics/?58967?yearbck=2022).

name: eSituation.16
label: Patient's Occupation
definition: The occupation of the patient.

allowed_values.display: Arts, Design, Entertainment, Sports, and Media Occupations
Building and Grounds Cleaning and Maintenance Occupations
Food Preparation and Serving Related Occupations
Life, Physical, and Social Science Occupations

element_comments: Based on the Standard Occupational Classification of the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (https://www.bls.gov/soc/2018/soc_structure_2018.pdf)
version3_changes: Added to better describe work related injury.

name: eSituation.17
label: Patient Activity
definition: The activity the patient was involved in at the time the patient experienced the onset of symptoms or experienced an injury.
element_comments: Code list is represented in ICD-10-CM. Reference the NEMSIS Suggested Lists at: https://nemsis.org/technical-resources/version-3/version-3-resource-repository/ Code list is represented in ICD-10 Codes. Website - http://uts.nlm.nih.gov Product - UMLS Metathesaurus

validation_rules.message: When Patient Activity is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Patient Activity has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
When Patient Activity has a Not Value, no other value should be recorded.

name: eSituation.18
label: Date/Time Last Known Well
definition: The estimated date and time the patient was last known to be well or in their usual state of health. This is described or estimated by the patient, family, and/or bystanders.
element_comments: For stroke related events, this is the date and time the patient was last seen normal. For cardiac or respiratory arrest related events, this is the date and time the patient was last known to have a pulse or when interaction was had with the patient. For drowning related events, this is the date and time the patient was last seen. For injury or trauma related events, this is the date and time the patient was injured.

validation_rules.message: When Date/Time Last Known Well is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Date/Time Last Known Well has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Destination Patient Transfer of Care Date/Time should not be earlier than Date/Time Last Known Well.
Unit Back in Service Date/Time should not be earlier than Date/Time Last Known Well.
Date/Time Last Known Well should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided" and Cardiac Arrest is "Yes...".
Date/Time Last Known Well should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided" and Stroke Scale Result is "Positive".
Date/Time Last Known Well should be recorded when Patient Evaluation/Care is "Patient Evaluated and Care Provided" and Cardiac Rhythm / Electrocardiography (ECG) is "STEMI...".
When Date/Time Last Known Well has a Pertinent Negative of "Unable to Complete", it should be empty and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).
When Date/Time Last Known Well has a Pertinent Negative of "Approximate", it should have a value and it should not have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting).

name: eSituation.19
label: Justification for Transfer or Encounter
definition: The ordering physician or medical provider diagnosis or stated reason for a hospital-to-hospital transfer, other medical transport, or Mobile Integrated Healthcare encounter.
element_comments: This is the diagnosis or reason for transfer, or medical transport or Integrated Healthcare Encounter provided by the ordering physician or medical provider. Hospital-to-hospital transfers and medical transports are based on the ordering medical provider, and not the EMS provider's impression. The text of the reason or diagnosis would be entered here rather than using the patient complaint field (as the patient may still have complaints to document), eSituation.11 Provider's Primary Impression or eSituation.12 Provider's Secondary Impressions. This element should only be documented when eResponse.05 Type of Service Requested is a transfer, Other Routine Medical Transport, or Mobile Integrated Health Care Encounter.

validation_rules.message: When Justification for Transfer or Encounter is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Justification for Transfer or Encounter has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

version3_changes: This element is being added with the release of v3.5.0

name: eSituation.20
label: Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport
definition: The general categories of the reason for an interfacility transfer/medical transport.
element_comments: This supports and works in combination with eSituation.19 Justification for Transfer or Encounter to provide defined categories for an interfacility transfer or other medical transport. This documentation provides support for reimbursement and a means for regions and states to evaluate transfer patterns and types.

validation_rules.message: When Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.

Reason for Interfacility Transfer/Medical Transport should only be recorded when Type of Service Requested is "... name: eTimes.01
label: PSAP Call Date/Time
definition: The date/time the phone rings (911 call to public safety answering point or other designated entity) requesting EMS services.
element_comments: This date/time might be the same as Dispatch Notified Date/Time (eTimes.02). DateTime consists of finite-length characters of the form: yyyy '-' mm '-' dd 'T' hh ':' mm ':' ss ('.' s+)? (zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

validation_rules.message: When PSAP Call Date/Time is empty, it should have a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting, if allowed for the element) or a Pertinent Negative (if allowed for the element), or it should be omitted (if the element is optional).
When PSAP Call Date/Time has a Not Value (Not Applicable, Not Recorded, or Not Reporting), it should be empty.
Unit Notified by Dispatch Date/Time should not be earlier than PSAP Call Date/Time.

(zzzzzz) yyyy a four-digit numeral that represents the year '-' separators between parts of the date portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the month dd a two-digit numeral that represents the day T separator that indicates time-of-day follows hh a two-digit numeral that represents the hour ':' a separator between parts of the time-of-day portion mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minute ss a two-integer-digit numeral that represents the whole seconds '.' s+ (not required) represents the fractional seconds zzzzzz (required) represents the timezone (as described below) Timezones, required, are durations with (integer-valued) hour and minute properties in the form: ('+' | '-') hh ':' mm hh a two-digit numeral (with leading zeros as required) that represents the hours mm a two-digit numeral that represents the minutes '+' a nonnegative duration '-' a nonpositive duration

name: eTimes.16
label: EMS Call Completed Date/Time
definition: The date/time the responding unit completed all tasks associated with the event including transfer of the patient, and such things as cleaning and restocking.
element_comments: For many EMS agencies, this would equal Unit Back in Service Date/Time (eTimes.13) if no travel is required to be back in service. element_comments: For use when the EMS Units and personnel are not allowed access to the scene until it is cleared for reasons such as safety (e.g., shooting, hazmat, tornado, fire, etc.).
